data.title: Eightco (NASDAQ: ORBS) Invests Additional $40 Million into OpenAI, Bringing Total OpenAI Investment to $90 Million
data.description: Around 30% of ORBS' entire treasury is currently held by OpenAI, a significant portion. ORBS is a unique firm that enables individual investors to invest in prominent private enterprises like OpenAI and Beast Industries. Tom Lee, who is the Chairman of Bitmine (NYSE: BMNR), has recently been appointed to ORBS' board of directors in order to provide support to the company.

data.title: PureCycle to Participate in 38th Annual ROTH Conference
data.description: Dustin Olson, the CEO of PureCycle, is set to partake in the upcoming 38th Annual ROTH Conference where he will discuss the latest updates regarding the company. This event is significant as it gathers numerous investors and key industry players, offering a valuable platform for networking and showcasing PureCycle's developments. Olson's attendance underscores PureCycle's commitment to engaging with stakeholders and enhancing visibility within the investment community. This opportunity will allow Olson to present crucial data and future prospects to a wide audience, potentially impacting the company's growth trajectory. The conference, known for attracting around 4,700 attendees and over 550 participating companies from various sectors, will provide PureCycle with a notable platform to communicate its strategic advancements and cement its position in the market.

data.title: Bags.fm Wins “Crowdfunding Innovation Award” in 2026 FinTech Breakthrough Awards
data.description: In Los Angeles, Bags.fm, a rapidly expanding global crypto-native launchpad for developers and creators, was declared the recipient of the "Crowdfunding Innovation Award" in the 10th edition of the FinTech Breakthrough Awards. This prestigious program acknowledges firms and technologies that are spearheading innovation in the worldwide financial technology sector. Bags.fm runs a token-powered launchpad that focuses on continual, internet-native engagement rather than singular activities.

data.title: New England State Chapters Unite to Advance Financial Education and Advisory Leadership
data.description: Boston, March 20, 2026 - The National Financial Educators Council® (NFEC) is pleased to announce the expansion of its network with state chapters in the New England region. These chapters now cover Connecticut, Maine, Massachusetts, New Hampshire, and Vermont. This growth demonstrates the NFEC's commitment to financial education, with a combined reach of over 500,000 individuals across these states. Through these state chapters, the NFEC aims to equip people with essential financial skills, promoting financial literacy and better financial decision-making.
